Name: Interstellar
Review:
Watched it like 5-6 years back on TV and today watched it on IMAX on 10th anniversary. This feeling is like can't tell in words. Cornfield chase, S.T.A.Y, docking I can still hear them in my ear.

A visually stunning and emotionally charged space odyssey. Nolan weaves mind-bending science with a deeply human story of love, sacrifice, and survival. McConaughey delivers a powerhouse performance, while Zimmer’s haunting score elevates every moment.

A masterpiece that explores both the vastness of space and the depth of human emotion.

Name: Children of Men
Review:
A hope in a hopeless world..!!

Watching Children of Men felt less like science fiction and more like a quiet warning. Cuarón builds a world in a near dystopian where humanity’s infertility mirrors a deeper loss of empathy, hope, and purpose. Clive Owen’s character, not a hero, just someone numb, trying to care again. The long, unbroken shots pulled me straight into the chaos. It’s bleak, yes, but somehow,quietly still human.

Name: Nayakan
Review: Was he a good man or a bad one? Maybe he wasn’t either. Maybe he was just human—trying to survive a world that never gave him a choice. A haunting saga of power, pain, and purpose. Kamal Haasan delivers one of Indian cinema’s finest performances, breathing soul into Velu Naicker with heartbreaking realism. The film doesn't just echo The Godfather—it becomes its own epic, rooted deeply in the streets of Bombay and the heart of a man torn between justice and violence. Mani Ratnam crafts a masterpiece that lingers long after the credits roll.

"Adjusting the seat, really?... that's been your fucking problem the whole time, the seat height..?" "And here comes mister gay pride of upper West side himself"
This is how perfection looks. I would allow whiplash to peg me. As i re-watching this, I really loving yellow colour more and more. This movie is like a violent love Letter to obsession. And that last 9 minutes solo drum..no dialogue. Just the rhythm of madness. You watch it and you don't breath for straight 9 minutes. And final shot, Andrew and fletcher's eyes locked. One smirking with pride. The other drenched in glory and madness. AND THEN-- BLACK SCREEN.

Name: Two
Review: No words. Just silence, privilege and a haunting message. 🎭 Ray turns simple face off between a rich kid and a poor boy into a quite war of pride. The rich kid "wins", but at what cost..?? The poor child's flute is silenced...and in the end, even rich kid's toy falls. Lifeless and forgotten. Power born from privilege can be loud, but never meaningful.

Name: F1
Review:
This wasn't a movie. It was an experience.
The vibration in seats, the screams of engines, Zinmer's score punching- pure adrenaline rush.

All watching it all unfold in IMAX..? Absolutely wild.

One of the best theater experience I have had.

Name: In the Mood for Love
Review:
Visually, this is pure poetry. Narrow corridors, reflection, foreground clutter. Everything feels tight like every characters are trapped inside a world.

That theme music...every time it played. It was like time slowed down. People walking in slow motion, drenched in regret.

Although the ending didn't completely hit me. May be it's one of those things u need to live through to really feel. It feels like someone else's story.

I think I will come back for this in few years,  when I have been through something like that, and this 4 will quietly turn into 5.
👍🏻👍🏻👍🏻

Name: Devi
Review:
Satyajit Ray once told in a interview:
"Once made a film called The goddess (Devi) . It dealt with religious dogmatism. It didn't attack religion as such. It attacked dogmatism, the extreme foam of religion. But people were writing in a paper that 'oh! because Mr. Ray is not a Hindu, he was a brahmo, he is making such films against Hinduism' and all that. But they r stupid people, u can't take them seriously into your account. And this happens in India all the time. And we have a fairly backward audience here. Inspite of a film society movement. If u consider a large audience, ita backward audience, unsophicated audience"

Name: Ship of Theseus
Review: “If every part of a ship is replaced, is it still the same ship?” Anand gandhi thank you for this..🙏 I finished Ship of Theseus and gave a two-minute standing ovation alone in my room, to the miracle of cinema itself. Some movies pass time, this one passed through me. Three lives, three philosophies. Aliya’s world moved me the most. no wonder Aida El-Kashef won Best Supporting Actress award. Then the debate between Maitreya and Charvaka. that conversation felt older than all of us, like two civilizations speaking. And Navin… starting with numbers and profit, slowly learning that conscience is heavier than any currency. I loved how gentle the film was with its ideas. No lectures, only people trying to understand what part of them is truly theirs and what is borrowed from the world. I don’t have perfect words. But yeah this is a masterpiece. a work of ART. I found another reason why I fell in love with CINEMA at first place.

Name: Marty Supreme
Review: Timothée absolutely carries this. chaotic, charming, and so alive on screen that, you stay with Marty till the end. Technically, the camera work is strong and the editing works well with the chaotic tone, but I watched Challengers just a week ago and my mind does automatically comparison. The music here is good, but in Challengers the score drives the tension; here it mostly supports it.

Name: Little Miss Sunshine
Review: I loved this so much..❤️🙌 It’s such a simple story, just a messy family going on a road trip. every character is flawed in their own way, but that’s what makes them special. No one is perfect, and the movie doesn’t try to make them heroes. The van, the awkward moments, the small fights; everything felt natural. It doesn’t try to be epic. It doesn’t try to be profound. It’s small. Awkward. Human.

Name: Desh Re Joya Dada Pardesh Joya
Review: Some films are better when you stop looking for logic. And that's the beauty of films. Watched it with my parents, kept asking them about scenes, words, and emotions, and somehow that made the whole experience even more special. The songs are beautiful, genuinely. Every single one.❤️❤️ The way they use such rich, heavy Gujarati words so naturally in simple conversations made me fall in love with it even more. જ્યાં આપણું જ લોહી આપણને છેતરતું હોય ત્યાં બીજા ને શું દોષ દેવો..?? મારા માળામાં રહેલા પંખીનું ગળું હું ન મરડી શકું. પિતા કદી પારધી ન બની શકે.

Name: Sangam
Review:
I liked it but not that much. I think my biggest mistake was watching the longest version on YouTube (3 hr 53 min) 😭

It felt too lengthy, especially after Sunder and Radha go on honeymoon. For like 25-30 mins the story doesn’t really move forward much.
Songs are great though.
And personally I feel first it’s Sunder’s fault because Radha is clearly not fully into it but he still keeps going after her. Then it’s Gopal’s fault too because he should have told Sunder the truth that he and Radha loved each other.
In the end I just felt bad for Radha because no one is really asking what she wants.

Name: Assi
Review: I’m kinda confused between 3 and 3.5. • solid film • important subject • good from child’s POV • but held back • could’ve been more brutally honest / more emotionally devastating / more courtroom-heavy / more fearless And all this is only about the movie. because the movie ends. Reality doesn’t. Becaue in reality, this issue is way darker and way messier in India. It has always been happening, and sadly it will keep happening even after this film too. In India, sometimes justice isn’t a right. It’s a privilege.
